title: eclipta erecta var. brachypoda
intro: Eclipta erecta var. brachypoda, commonly known as Eclipta, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, often used as a hair growth stimulant and for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Eclipta is valued for its potential to promote hair growth, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its health benefits. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support hair care, skin health, and overall well-being. Eclipta is also sometimes referred to as false daisy or baldmoney, and its extracts are used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat a variety of health conditions.
